# NVforHD — Nevada for Huntington's Disease

> NVforHD is a Nevada non-profit that raises awareness and funds to battle Huntington's Disease through an annual charity golf tournament. 100% of proceeds go to one chosen HD cause each year.

## Organisation

- **Name:** NVforHD (Nevada for HD)
- **Type:** Non-profit organisation
- **Founded:** 2024
- **Location:** Reno, Nevada
- **Event venue:** Gray's Crossing Golf Club, Truckee, CA
- **Phone:** 775-691-8860 (Sean Schaeffer, Founder)
- **Email:** info@nvforhd.com
- **Website:** https://www.nvforhd.com

## Mission

Every year NVforHD hosts a charity golf tournament and directs 100% of proceeds to one chosen Huntington's Disease cause — supporting treatment, awareness, or an eventual cure. Zero overhead. One cause. Full transparency.

## Impact

- **$50,000+** raised in first two years
- **2024 beneficiary:** HelpCureHD — funded IVF for Brandon & Rylee Puccini, enabling an HD-free baby
- **2025 & 2026 beneficiary:** UC Davis HD Center of Excellence (Sacramento, CA)
- **90+ Northern Nevada families** served by the UC Davis HD clinic

## The Puccini Story — 2024 Impact

Brandon and Rylee Puccini are the human proof of what NVforHD does. The 2024 tournament raised $25,000 for HelpCureHD. Those funds paid for IVF with preimplantation genetic testing, allowing Brandon and Rylee to have a baby free from Huntington's Disease. Their thank-you letter — "Thank you for gifting us with the miracle of life, and specifically life that is Huntington's free" — is published on the NVforHD website. The baby will never carry the HD gene.

## Annual Event — 2026

- **Date:** Friday, May 29, 2026
- **Venue:** Gray's Crossing Golf Club, Truckee, CA (Peter Jacobsen design, 6,000ft elevation)
- **Format:** Four-person scramble, 12:00 PM shotgun start, lunch included
- **Hotel partner:** Atlantis & Peppermill, Reno — discount rooms available, call Sean 775-691-8860

### 2026 Packages
- Title Sponsor: $3,500 (Sold Out) — 4 golfers + lunch + logo at all events
- Gold Sponsor: $3,000 (Sold Out) — 4 golfers + lunch + company logo
- Lunch Sponsor: $3,000 — 4 golfers + lunch sponsor credit
- Silver/Remote Sponsor: $500 — support without attending
- Golf Foursome: $880 — golf + lunch for 4
- Hole Sign Sponsor: $100 — name on course
- Direct Donation: $100 minimum
- Book: https://www.tripsee.travel/merchant/book/index.html?ref=2026NVforHDGolfTournament

## About Huntington's Disease

Huntington's Disease (HD) is a hereditary, fatal brain disorder with no known cure. It causes progressive breakdown of nerve cells — likened to having Parkinson's, ALS, and Alzheimer's simultaneously. Every child of an HD parent has a 50% chance of inheriting it. Approximately 1 in 100,000 people globally have HD. There is no treatment that slows the disease.

## UC Davis HD Center of Excellence — 2025 & 2026 Beneficiary

The UC Davis Huntington's Disease Center of Excellence in Sacramento is the only HD specialty clinic within reach of Northern Nevada families. It serves 90+ families in the Reno-Sparks metro and Sierra Foothills region — over 130 miles away.

### The Team
- 2 Movement Disorder Neurologists specialising in HD
- 2 Psychiatrists with HD-specific training
- 1 HD Genetic Counselor
- 1 HD Social Worker
- 2 Physical Therapists with neuro specialty and HD experience
- 3 Research Coordinators (grant-funded)
- Neuropsychologist (added 2025)
- OB/GYN HD specialists (added 2025)

### Director
**Dr. Alexandra (Sasha) Duffy, DO** — Clinical Professor, Vice Chair of Education, Adult Neurology Residency Program Director, and Director of the UC Davis HD Center of Excellence, Department of Neurology. She has led the clinic for over 32 years alongside predecessor Dr. Wheelock.

The clinic operates entirely on private donation. It runs clinical research trials and actively contributes to the science of HD treatment and eventual cure.

## Board Members

- **Sean Schaeffer** — Founder. 30+ years in sales and customer service. CEO of publicly traded technology company. Wife Christine was diagnosed with Huntington's Disease — the personal reason NVforHD exists.
- **Mike Milligan** — Board Member. Director of Sales, Golf the High Sierra. Former President of Golf the High Sierra 2005–2014. Reno/Lake Tahoe golf industry since 2004.
- **Michael R. Eskuchen** — Board Member. Account Manager, Golf the High Sierra. Former GM & COO of Hidden Valley CC, Reno, NV.
- **John McGinnes** — Board Member. Executive Director of Sales, Atlantis Casino Resort Spa, Reno. 30 years in hotel sales.

## Patient Families

- **Tiffany** — 24-year HD journey. Husband showed symptoms in 2005. Travels 2 hours each way to UC Davis. "They didn't just treat the medical side. They cared about the whole journey."
- **Mikey & Holly** — Holly diagnosed October 2023 at UC Davis. "Their care for us is not transactional. They are in this journey with us." Named Dr. Duffy, Dr. Campos, Dr. Hart, and Lisa Mooney as the team making the most difference.
- **Leilani Dunmoyer** — Mother diagnosed 16 years ago. Leilani is gene-positive. Brother has HD. Three children at risk. Participates in clinical trials and runs marathons to raise HD awareness. "We just felt hopeless and helpless — until we found UC Davis."

## Partners

- [HelpCureHD](http://www.helpcurehd.com) — 2024 beneficiary. IVF funding for HD families. Founded by Joe Smith and Allie LaForce to honour Lee Smith who died of HD in 2020.
- [UC Davis HD Center of Excellence](https://health.ucdavis.edu/huntingtons-disease) — 2025 & 2026 beneficiary.
- [Golf the High Sierra](https://www.golfthehighsierra.com) — Tournament logistics partner.
